speaker: PORTIA
text: Go, draw aside the curtains and discover

speaker: PORTIA
text: The several caskets to this noble prince.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Now make your choice.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: This first, of gold, who this inscription bears,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: “Who chooseth me shall gain what many men desire”;

speaker: MOROCCO
text: The second, silver, which this promise carries,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: “Who chooseth me shall get as much as he deserves”;

speaker: MOROCCO
text: This third, dull lead, with warning all as blunt,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: “Who chooseth me must give and hazard all he hath.”

speaker: MOROCCO
text: How shall I know if I do choose the right?

speaker: PORTIA
text: The one of them contains my picture, prince.

speaker: PORTIA
text: If you choose that, then I am yours withal.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Some god direct my judgment! Let me see.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: I will survey th’ inscriptions back again.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: What says this leaden casket?

speaker: MOROCCO
text: “Who chooseth me must give and hazard all he hath.”

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Must give—for what? For lead? Hazard for lead?

speaker: MOROCCO
text: This casket threatens. Men that hazard all

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Do it in hope of fair advantages.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: A golden mind stoops not to shows of dross.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: I’ll then nor give nor hazard aught for lead.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: What says the silver with her virgin hue?

speaker: MOROCCO
text: “Who chooseth me shall get as much as he deserves.”

speaker: MOROCCO
text: As much as he deserves—pause there, Morocco,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: And weigh thy value with an even hand.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: If thou beest rated by thy estimation,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Thou dost deserve enough; and yet enough

speaker: MOROCCO
text: May not extend so far as to the lady.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: And yet to be afeard of my deserving

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Were but a weak disabling of myself.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: As much as I deserve—why, that’s the lady!

speaker: MOROCCO
text: I do in birth deserve her, and in fortunes,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: In graces, and in qualities of breeding,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: But more than these, in love I do deserve.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: What if I strayed no farther, but chose here?

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Let’s see once more this saying graved in gold:

speaker: MOROCCO
text: “Who chooseth me shall gain what many men desire.”

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Why, that’s the lady! All the world desires her.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: From the four corners of the Earth they come

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To kiss this shrine, this mortal, breathing saint.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: The Hyrcanian deserts and the vasty wilds

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Of wide Arabia are as throughfares now

speaker: MOROCCO
text: For princes to come view fair Portia.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: The watery kingdom, whose ambitious head

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Spets in the face of heaven, is no bar

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To stop the foreign spirits, but they come

speaker: MOROCCO
text: As o’er a brook to see fair Portia.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: One of these three contains her heavenly picture.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Is ’t like that lead contains her? ’Twere damnation

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To think so base a thought. It were too gross

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To rib her cerecloth in the obscure grave.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Or shall I think in silver she’s immured,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Being ten times undervalued to tried gold?

speaker: MOROCCO
text: O, sinful thought! Never so rich a gem

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Was set in worse than gold. They have in England

speaker: MOROCCO
text: A coin that bears the figure of an angel

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Stamped in gold, but that’s insculped upon;

speaker: MOROCCO
text: But here an angel in a golden bed

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Lies all within.—Deliver me the key.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Here do I choose, and thrive I as I may.

speaker: PORTIA
text:
There, take it, prince.
                 
                Handing
                 
                him
                 
                the
                 
                key
                .
               And if my form lie there,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Then I am yours.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: O hell! What have we here?

speaker: MOROCCO
text: A carrion death within whose empty eye

speaker: MOROCCO
text: There is a written scroll. I’ll read the writing:

speaker: MOROCCO
text: All that glisters is not gold—

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Often have you heard that told.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Many a man his life hath sold

speaker: MOROCCO
text: But my outside to behold.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Gilded tombs do worms infold.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Had you been as wise as bold,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Young in limbs, in judgment old,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Your answer had not been enscrolled.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Fare you well, your suit is cold.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Cold indeed and labor lost!

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Then, farewell, heat, and welcome, frost.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Portia, adieu. I have too grieved a heart

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To take a tedious leave. Thus losers part.

speaker: PORTIA
text: A gentle riddance! Draw the curtains, go.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Let all of his complexion choose me so.
